text: Those aren’t puddles… they’re pothole camouflage.
Slow down. The scenery is worth arriving for.
A few extra minutes can save a tyre.
Single-track roads work best when everyone is patient.
Some puddles are simply potholes wearing raincoats.
If the road is wet, give the potholes a little more respect.
The sheep were here first. Please drive accordingly.
Highland roads reward patience more than speed.
The weather changes every day. The beauty of Lochcarron never does.
There is no bad weather, only different Highland scenery.
Sunshine is wonderful. Mist is magical.
If you can see the hills, enjoy the view. If you can’t, wait ten minutes.
Rain brings the waterfalls to life.
A dry day in the Highlands is a gift. Make the most of it.
The forecast is only the beginning of the story.
Grey skies can still make golden memories.
The best photographs are often taken just after the rain stops.
Clouds often make the most dramatic Highland photographs.
Sometimes the best view is behind you.
Mist can hide the hills, but it often reveals the mood.
Sunset is worth waiting for, even when the day looked ordinary.
A camera captures the view. A quiet moment captures the memory.
Good boots are remembered long after fashionable shoes are forgotten.
A waterproof jacket weighs far less than regret.
Take only photographs. Leave only footprints.
The best walks are not always the longest ones.
Pack for the weather you might get, not just the weather you hope for.
A flask of tea has rescued many Highland plans.
Around here, light rain still means waterproofs.
In Lochcarron, the weather likes to keep its options open.
Today’s weather may contain traces of yesterday’s forecast.
The hills are not hiding. They’re just being dramatic.
If the sheep look dry, you might be safe for five minutes.
Sunshine in the Highlands is best enjoyed immediately.
Every season paints the Highlands differently.
Listen carefully. Sometimes the quiet is the best part.
The tide, the wind and the hills all keep their own timetable.
Wild places are best enjoyed with respect.
The Highlands do not need perfect weather to be beautiful.
A calm loch can make the whole day feel slower.
Clear skies are only half the story. The Sun writes the rest.
Patience is often rewarded beneath Highland skies.
For the Northern Lights, look north and give your eyes time to adjust.
The best aurora moments often arrive when you nearly gave up waiting.
You do not visit the Highlands to hurry.
Keep your fuel tank topped up. The next stop may be farther than you think.
The best days often start without much of a plan.
Some roads are not shortcuts. They are part of the adventure.
Wave, smile and take your time. It works well on Highland roads.
The Highlands are easier to enjoy when you stop measuring time.
